title: Dess-Martin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dess-Martin oxidation describing Dess-Martin periodinane (DMP), a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, as a mild and selective oxidant for 1° and 2° alcohols and oximes to the corresponding carbonyl compounds.
notes: Key concepts: IBX to DMP reagent preparation, hypervalent iodine oxidation, mild neutral conditions, aldehyde/ketone formation, alkoxyperiodinane intermediates.

entities.notes: Named hypervalent-iodine oxidation family overview.
Soluble iodine(V) oxidant central to the named reaction.
Canonical aldehyde/ketone product class formed from alcohol or oxime oxidation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidation of alcohols or oximes to carbonyl compounds with hypervalent iodine(V) reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Mild and chemoselective oxidation using Dess-Martin periodinane, a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, to convert alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alcohol oxidation to aldehydes or ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Low-temperature oxidation of alcohols with DMSO activated by oxalyl chloride or related reagents to give aldehydes or ketones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enolate or heteroatom oxidation by N-sulfonyloxaziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation chemistry of N-sulfonyloxaziridines, especially alpha-hydroxylation of enolates and selective oxygen transfer to sulfides or other nucleophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-mediated dehydrogenation of silyl enol ethers to enones and enals
similar_existing.description_short: Named oxidation that converts silyl enol ethers into conjugated enones or enals under Pd(II) conditions.

title: Dieckmann Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dieckmann condensation describing intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters and the related Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization of dinitriles to enamino nitriles under strong base.
notes: Key concepts: intramolecular Claisen condensation, cyclic beta-keto ester formation, Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization, reversible enolate-driven ring closure, ring-size effects.

entities.notes: Named intramolecular Claisen condensation family overview.
Related nitrile cyclization showcased alongside the Dieckmann condensation.
Canonical product class from intramolecular Claisen ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters
similar_existing.description_short: Intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters that yields cyclic beta-keto esters, often complemented by related nitrile cyclizations in ring-building synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: LDA, THF | reagent; NaCN, DMSO | reagent | NaOEt, THF / EtOH | reagent; Me3SiOK, THF, then HCl | reagent; BH3·SMe2, THF, then EtOH/HCl | reagent; NaH, LiBH4, 1,4-dioxane, then 0.5 M HCl | reagent | LDA, THF, HMPA, ZnCl2-ether | reagent; CSA, CH(OMe)3, MeOH, CH2Cl2 | reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Mixed Claisen Condensation

similar_existing.transformation_type: ester reductive dimerization to α-hydroxy ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Acyloin condensation reductively couples esters to acyloins and is especially valuable for cyclic and intramolecular acyloin formation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-mediated condensation of carbonyl compounds with dialkyl succinates
similar_existing.description_short: Condensation of aldehydes or ketones with dialkyl succinates to furnish alkylidene succinic half-esters after acidification.

similar_existing.transformation_type: amine- or base-catalyzed condensation of aldehydes or ketones with active methylene compounds to electron-poor alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Condensation of carbonyl compounds with active methylene donors under basic or amine catalysis to form alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl derivatives and related activated alkenes.

title: Diels-Alder Cycloaddition – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Diels-Alder cycloaddition describing the concerted [4+2] cycloaddition of dienes with alkenes or alkynes to form cyclohexene derivatives, including normal and inverse electron-demand modes, regioselectivity, endo preference, and stereospecificity.
notes: Key concepts: concerted pericyclic reaction, endo rule, normal versus inverse electron-demand Diels-Alder reactivity, ortho/para regiocontrol, aromatic transition state.

entities.notes: Named [4+2] cycloaddition family overview.
Electron-rich diene/electron-poor dienophile mode emphasized on the overview page.
Representative regiochemical outcome shown in the overview schemes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: concerted [4+2] cycloaddition of diene and dienophile to cyclohexene derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Concerted [4+2] cycloaddition that rapidly generates six-membered rings with predictable stereo- and regioselectivity in normal or inverse electron-demand modes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: heteroatom-containing Diels-Alder [4+2] cycloaddition
similar_existing.description_short: Pericyclic or stepwise hetero-[4+2] annulation used to assemble densely functionalized oxygen- and nitrogen-heterocycles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: cycloaddition
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

similar_existing.transformation_type: photochemical enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition with optional retro-aldol fragmentation
similar_existing.description_short: Photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes that can furnish cyclobutanes directly or 1,5-diketones after retro-aldol fragmentation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: [2+2] cycloaddition of ketenes with alkenes, imines, or carbonyls
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal or Lewis acid assisted [2+2] cycloaddition chemistry of ketenes that furnishes four-membered rings.

title: Dienone-Phenol Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the dienone-phenol rearrangement describing acid- or base-promoted migration of alkyl groups in cyclohexadienones to aromatic phenols, including ortho- and para-dienone cases and sequential 1,2-shift pathways that deliver highly substituted phenols.
notes: Key concepts: dienone rearomatization, alkyl migration, ortho versus para substitution patterns, 1,2-shift cascades, acid-catalyzed rearrangement of santonin-type frameworks.

entities.notes: Named rearomatizing alkyl-migration family overview.
Overall migration pattern often rationalized as sequential 1,2-shifts in cyclohexadienone systems.
Representative rearomatized product class from para-dienone rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-, base-, or photochemically induced alkyl migration in cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement of cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols driven by rearomatization, often proceeding through sequential 1,2-shifts and sensitive to substitution pattern and reaction conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: tandem Prins cyclization and pinacol rearrangement to build tetrahydrofuran frameworks
similar_existing.description_short: Powerful tandem cationic cascade that merges Prins cyclization with pinacol rearrangement to forge densely substituted tetrahydrofuran and annulated ring systems.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ring-opening / ring-closing heterocycle isomerization with heteroatom translocation
similar_existing.description_short: Ring-opening / ring-closing rearrangement of heterocycles that translocates heteroatoms and attached substituents to deliver isomeric aza-heterocycles or rearranged nucleosides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-induced rearrangement of O-acyl ketoximes to alpha-amino ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Method for transforming oximes into alpha-amino ketones via O-acyl activation and alkoxide-promoted rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of tertiary amine N-oxides to hydroxylamines or ring-expanded heterocycles
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement manifold of tertiary amine N-oxides that furnishes hydroxylamines or ring-expanded azaheterocycles via [1,2]- or [2,3]-shifts.

title: Doering-Laflamme Allene Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Doering-Laflamme allene synthesis describing two-step one-carbon homologation of alkenes through dihalocarbene addition to form geminal dihalocyclopropanes followed by metal-mediated rearrangement to acyclic or cyclic allenes.
notes: Key concepts: in situ dihalocarbene generation from haloform, cyclopropanation of alkenes, metal–halogen exchange, and cyclopropylidene-to-allene rearrangement.

entities.notes: Named allene-synthesis family overview.
Key cyclopropane intermediate generated by dihalocarbene addition.
Canonical cyclic allene product class shown in the overview scheme.

similar_existing.transformation_type: one-carbon homologation of alkenes to allenes via dihalocyclopropane intermediates
similar_existing.description_short: Classical allene synthesis in which dihalocarbene addition to an alkene gives a geminal dihalocyclopropane that is rearranged under metal-mediated conditions to an allene.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aldehyde one-carbon homologation to terminal alkyne
similar_existing.description_short: One-carbon homologation of aldehydes to terminal alkynes through dibromoolefin intermediates and organolithium-mediated elimination.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enamine-mediated alpha-functionalization of carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Use of enamines as enolate equivalents for alpha-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, and related carbonyl functionalization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: controlled acyl substitution to ketones or aldehydes
similar_existing.description_short: Preparation and use of N-methoxy-N-methylamides to convert carboxylic acid derivatives into ketones or aldehydes with single-addition control.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

title: Evans Aldol Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Evans aldol reaction describing boron-mediated, chiral auxiliary-controlled aldol addition of N-acyl oxazolidinones to aldehydes through Z-boron enolates, delivering syn aldol adducts with high diastereoselectivity via Zimmerman-Traxler transition states.
notes: Key concepts: Bu2BOTf-mediated enolization of oxazolidinones, Z-enolate control, Zimmerman-Traxler transition-state model, and syn-selective carbon–carbon bond formation using Evans auxiliaries.

entities.notes: Named boron-mediated aldol family overview.
Boron enolate geometry that controls the syn-selective aldol addition.
Chairlike transition-state model used to rationalize the observed syn diastereoselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: auxiliary-controlled syn-selective aldol addition
similar_existing.description_short: Highly diastereoselective syn aldol reaction of chiral N-acyl oxazolidinones with aldehydes through Z-boron enolates and Zimmerman-Traxler transition states.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enolate addition to carbonyl / β-hydroxy carbonyl formation
similar_existing.description_short: The aldol reaction forges C–C bonds between enol/enolate donors and carbonyl electrophiles to produce β-hydroxy carbonyl compounds, often with high stereocontrol.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted aldol coupling of silyl enol ethers with carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Powerful aldol variant using preformed enol silanes and Lewis acid activation to deliver beta-hydroxy carbonyl products with high stereocontrol.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted halogenation and cleavage of methyl ketones or methyl carbinols to haloforms and carboxylates
similar_existing.description_short: One-carbon degradation method that converts methyl ketones and related methyl carbinols into haloforms and carboxylate products under hypohalite conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aldehyde disproportionation / hydride transfer redox
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: concentrated NaOH, KOH, Lewis acid variants, aldehydes lacking alpha-hydrogen
similar_existing.description_short: Disproportionation of non-enolizable aldehydes to alcohols and carboxylates through hydride transfer under basic conditions.

title: Favorskii and Homo-Favorskii Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Favorskii and homo-Favorskii rearrangements describing skeletal rearrangement of alpha-halo ketones and ring-expanded or ring-contracted beta-halo ketones under basic conditions, often via cyclopropanone or cyclobutanone intermediates that are trapped by external nucleophiles.
notes: Key concepts: alpha-halo ketone rearrangement, cyclopropanone and cyclobutanone intermediates, regioselective ring opening, nucleophile trapping, and distinction between classical Favorskii, homo-Favorskii, and quasi-Favorskii pathways.

entities.notes: Named alpha-halo-ketone rearrangement family overview.
Ring-expanded beta-halo ketone variant highlighted on the overview page.
Characteristic mechanistic intermediate invoked for classical Favorskii rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: skeletal rearrangement of alpha-halo ketones to carboxylic-acid derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Base-promoted rearrangement of alpha-halo ketones that proceeds through cyclopropanone-like intermediates to give rearranged carboxylic-acid derivatives or contracted rings.

similar_existing.transformation_type: skeletal rearrangement of α-halo ketones to acids or ring-contracted carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Favorskii-like rearrangement manifold for α-halo ketones that cannot enolize, delivering carboxylic acid derivatives or ring-contracted products through a semibenzilic pathway.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: p-TsOH | Cc1ccc(S(=O)(=O)O)cc1; toluene | Cc1ccccc1; reflux | dry pyridine | c1ccncc1; AcOH | CC(=O)O; 24h, r.t.
similar_existing.description_short: Amadori Reaction

similar_existing.transformation_type: acyl migration from phenolic esters or carbamates to aromatic carbon
similar_existing.description_short: Family of acyl-migration reactions that translocate acyl groups from oxygen to carbon on aromatic systems under ionic, radical, or anionic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: anionic silyl migration / annulation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: alkoxide or organolithium generation, acyl silanes, THF
similar_existing.description_short: Anionic migration of silyl groups from carbon to oxygen that enables rearrangement and annulation logic.

title: Feist-Bénary Furan Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Feist-Bénary furan synthesis describing base-mediated condensation of beta-keto esters with alpha-halogenated carbonyl compounds to form substituted furans, often through isolable dihydrofuranol or 1,4-dicarbonyl intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: beta-keto ester enolate addition, intramolecular substitution, interrupted Feist-Bénary variants, acid-promoted dehydration, and access to highly substituted furan products.

entities.notes: Named furan-forming condensation family overview.
Variant that isolates oxygenated dihydrofuranol intermediates before dehydration.
Canonical heteroaromatic product class delivered by the Feist-Bénary condensation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: assembly of substituted furans from beta-keto esters and alpha-halo carbonyls
similar_existing.description_short: Base-mediated condensation that converts beta-keto esters and alpha-halocarbonyl compounds into substituted furans, often via dihydrofuranol intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: cyclodehydration of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds or surrogates to substituted furans
similar_existing.description_short: Classical heterocycle-forming reaction that transforms 1,4-dicarbonyl systems into furans by acid- or dehydrating-agent-mediated cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enamine-mediated alpha-functionalization of carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Use of enamines as enolate equivalents for alpha-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, and related carbonyl functionalization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aldehyde one-carbon homologation to terminal alkyne
similar_existing.description_short: One-carbon homologation of aldehydes to terminal alkynes through dibromoolefin intermediates and organolithium-mediated elimination.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alkylation of malonic esters followed by hydrolysis and decarboxylation to substituted acetic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational active-methylene strategy that converts alkylated malonate esters into substituted acetic acids and related cyclic products.

title: Ferrier Reaction / Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Ferrier reaction / rearrangement describing Lewis-acid-promoted conversion of unsaturated carbohydrate derivatives into 2,3-unsaturated glycosides or highly substituted cyclohexanones through allyloxycarbenium or ketoaldehyde intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: Type I Ferrier reaction, Type II Ferrier rearrangement, allyloxycarbenium formation, nucleophilic addition to glycals, and aldol-like cyclization of enol ethers to cyclohexanones.

entities.notes: Named carbohydrate rearrangement family overview.
Nucleophilic addition to glycals through allyloxycarbenium ions.
Enol-ether rearrangement route to highly substituted cyclohexanones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted conversion of glycals or enol ethers to unsaturated glycosides or cyclohexanones
similar_existing.description_short: Lewis-acid-promoted rearrangement chemistry of unsaturated carbohydrates that affords 2,3-unsaturated glycosides or highly substituted cyclohexanones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-promoted conversion of nitriles and carbocations into amides
similar_existing.description_short: Classical carbocation-trapping reaction in which nitriles are converted into amides under strongly acidic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or azlactone precursors to α,β-unsaturated acids or azlactones
similar_existing.description_short: Classical olefination manifold that converts aromatic aldehydes into cinnamic-acid-type products or azlactone intermediates using anhydride-based carbon nucleophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-mediated conversion of nitriles and alcohols or thiols into imino ether or imino thioether salts and downstream amidines or esters
similar_existing.description_short: Classical nitrile activation method that turns nitriles into imino ether salts and provides a versatile gateway to amidines, esters, and related nitrogen-rich motifs.

title: Finkelstein Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Finkelstein reaction describing halide exchange in alkyl halides driven by differential solubility of sodium halides in polar aprotic solvents, most often exchanging chlorides or bromides for iodides under SN2 conditions.
notes: Key concepts: sodium iodide in acetone, precipitation-driven equilibrium, SN2 halogen exchange, behavior of primary versus hindered substrates, and related conversion of sulfonates or pseudohalides.

entities.notes: Named halogen-exchange family overview.
Core substitution process in which one halide is replaced by another under SN2 conditions.
Mechanistic model used to describe the halide displacement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: halogen exchange in alkyl halides
similar_existing.description_short: Classical SN2 halogen-exchange reaction in which sodium iodide or related salts convert alkyl halides or sulfonates into the corresponding iodides or bromides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: multicomponent heterocycle synthesis
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: HCl, FeCl3, BF3·OEt2, Yb(OTf)3, urea, thiourea
similar_existing.description_short: Three-component condensation used to build dihydropyrimidinones from aldehydes, beta-keto esters, and urea derivatives.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical substitution of protonated electron-deficient heteroarenes
similar_existing.description_short: Radical-based alkylation or acylation of electron-poor heteroarenes that provides direct C-H functionalization under oxidative acidic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alpha-amino acid to alpha-acylamino ketone
similar_existing.description_short: Transformation of alpha-amino acids into alpha-acylamino ketones under anhydride/base conditions through mixed anhydride and oxazolone intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

title: Fischer Indole Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Fischer indole synthesis describing acid- or Lewis-acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones derived from ketones or aldehydes to furnish substituted indoles through ene-hydrazine rearrangement and ring closure.
notes: Key concepts: hydrazone formation, ene-hydrazine tautomerization, [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement, proton-transfer-driven cyclization, and the role of acidity and substitution in regiocontrol.

entities.notes: Named indolization family overview.
Arylhydrazone-forming precursor reaction explicitly cited on the overview page.
Key precursor class that undergoes acid-catalyzed Fischer indolization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed conversion of arylhydrazones into substituted indoles through tautomerization, sigmatropic rearrangement, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulative synthesis of 5-hydroxyindoles from benzoquinones and enamine partners
similar_existing.description_short: Benzoquinone-based indole annulation that furnishes substituted 5-hydroxyindoles from amino-enone or enamine partners.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: substituted nitroarene or nitrosoarene to indole using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagent

similar_existing.description_short: The Bartoli indole synthesis rapidly converts ortho-substituted nitroarenes into indoles or azaindoles using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

title: Fleming-Tamao Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Fleming-Tamao oxidation describing conversion of carbon-silicon bonds into alcohols under oxidative conditions through Tamao-type or Fleming-type protocols, often with retention of configuration at the carbon center.
notes: Key concepts: oxidative cleavage of C–Si bonds, peroxide oxidation, ipso substitution at heteroaryl silanes, hypervalent-silicon intermediates, and stereospecific access to alcohols from organosilanes.

entities.notes: Named oxidation family overview.
Core oxidative desilylation concept explicitly highlighted on the overview page.
General transformation converting organosilanes into alcohols.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidative conversion of organosilanes to alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidative conversion of carbon-silicon bonds into alcohols, often stereospecifically and with broad functional-group tolerance in complex synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-mediated dehydrogenation of silyl enol ethers to enones and enals
similar_existing.description_short: Named oxidation that converts silyl enol ethers into conjugated enones or enals under Pd(II) conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidation of alcohols or oximes to carbonyl compounds with hypervalent iodine(V) reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Mild and chemoselective oxidation using Dess-Martin periodinane, a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, to convert alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

title: Friedel-Crafts Acylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Friedel-Crafts acylation describing Lewis-acid-promoted installation of acyl groups onto aromatic or heteroaromatic substrates using acyl halides, anhydrides, or related acyl donors through acylium-ion-like electrophiles.
notes: Key concepts: acylium ion generation, Lewis acid activation, aromatic acylation versus aliphatic acylation, deactivation after first acylation, and utility of intramolecular variants for ring closure.

entities.notes: Named electrophilic aromatic acylation overview.
Canonical electrophile generated by Lewis-acid activation of the acyl donor.
Ring-closing acylation variant explicitly emphasized on the overview page.

similar_existing.description_short: Classical electrophilic aromatic acylation using Lewis-acid-activated acyl donors, widely used for intermolecular substitution and intramolecular ring-closing acylation.

similar_existing.description_short: Catalytic alkylation of electron-rich arenes or heteroarenes by carbocationic electrophiles generated from halides, alkenes, or alcohol derivatives.

similar_existing.description_short: Concerted [4+2] cycloaddition that rapidly generates six-membered rings with predictable stereo- and regioselectivity in normal or inverse electron-demand modes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: peroxyacid epoxidation of alkenes

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted conversion of glycals or enol ethers to unsaturated glycosides or cyclohexanones
similar_existing.description_short: Lewis-acid-promoted rearrangement chemistry of unsaturated carbohydrates that affords 2,3-unsaturated glycosides or highly substituted cyclohexanones.

title: Friedel-Crafts Alkylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Friedel-Crafts alkylation describing Lewis- or Brønsted-acid-promoted installation of alkyl groups onto aromatic or heteroaromatic substrates from alkyl halides, alkenes, alcohols, or related electrophiles through carbocationic activation.
notes: Key concepts: catalyst-dependent carbocation formation, rearrangements of primary and secondary alkyl groups, polyalkylation, hydroarylation variants, and sensitivity to electron demand and substituent effects on the aromatic ring.

entities.notes: Named electrophilic aromatic alkylation overview.
Electrophilic cationic intermediate responsible for aromatic substitution.
Variant in which alkenes or alcohol derivatives act as alkylating equivalents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: electrophilic aromatic alkylation
similar_existing.description_short: Catalytic alkylation of electron-rich arenes or heteroarenes by carbocationic electrophiles generated from halides, alkenes, or alcohol derivatives.

similar_existing.description_short: Concerted [4+2] cycloaddition that rapidly generates six-membered rings with predictable stereo- and regioselectivity in normal or inverse electron-demand modes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted allylation of carbonyl compounds with allylsilanes
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon–carbon bond-forming allylation of carbonyl compounds using allylsilanes under Lewis-acid activation.

title: Fries, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Fries-type rearrangements describing migration of acyl groups within phenolic esters or O-aryl carbamates under Lewis-acid, photochemical, or anionic conditions to furnish ortho- or para-acylated phenols and salicylamide derivatives.
notes: Key concepts: Lewis-acid-promoted acyl migration, photochemical photo-Fries rearrangement, anionic ortho-Fries via ortho-lithiated carbamates, and regioselectivity between ortho and para acylation products.

entities.notes: Named acyl-migration family overview.
Photochemical variant explicitly called out on the overview page.
Organolithium-driven ortho-acyl migration variant shown on the overview page.

similar_existing.description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: skeletal rearrangement of alpha-halo ketones to carboxylic-acid derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Base-promoted rearrangement of alpha-halo ketones that proceeds through cyclopropanone-like intermediates to give rearranged carboxylic-acid derivatives or contracted rings.

title: Gabriel Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Gabriel synthesis describing preparation of primary amines by alkylation of potassium phthalimide followed by hydrolysis or hydrazinolysis, including the Ing-Manske deprotection variant and related Gabriel malonic-ester logic.
notes: Key concepts: SN2 alkylation of phthalimide, hydrazinolysis, strongly basic hydrolysis, suitability for primary alkyl electrophiles, and mild deprotection variants used in complex-molecule synthesis.

entities.notes: Named phthalimide-based primary-amine synthesis overview.
Hydrazinolysis-based deprotection variant cited on the overview page.
Masked-ammonia reagent central to the Gabriel synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: conversion of alkyl electrophiles into primary amines via phthalimide masking
similar_existing.description_short: Two-step primary-amine synthesis in which phthalimide serves as a masked ammonia equivalent for SN2 alkylation and subsequent deprotection.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: substituted nitroarene or nitrosoarene to indole using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagent
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: vinylmagnesium bromide|alkenyl Grignard|THF|-40 to -78 C|acidic work-up|nitroarene
similar_existing.description_short: The Bartoli indole synthesis rapidly converts ortho-substituted nitroarenes into indoles or azaindoles using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enamine-mediated alpha-functionalization of carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Use of enamines as enolate equivalents for alpha-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, and related carbonyl functionalization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed conversion of arylhydrazones into substituted indoles through tautomerization, sigmatropic rearrangement, and ring closure.

title: Gattermann and Gattermann-Koch Formylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Gattermann and Gattermann-Koch formylation describing electrophilic introduction of formyl groups onto activated arenes using either CO/HCl with Lewis-acid carriers or HCN/Zn(CN)2-derived iminium equivalents under strongly Lewis-acidic conditions.
notes: Key concepts: arene formylation under Friedel-Crafts-like conditions, distinction between Gattermann-Koch and Gattermann variants, use of CO or cyanide surrogates, and limits imposed by substrate activation and harsh acidic media.

entities.notes: Named aromatic formylation family overview.
Carbon-monoxide-based formylation variant highlighted on the overview page.
Generic product class generated by electrophilic aromatic formylation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: electrophilic aromatic formylation of activated arenes
similar_existing.description_short: Classical aromatic formylation family using either carbon monoxide/hydrogen chloride or cyanide-derived formylation equivalents under Lewis-acidic conditions.

title: Glaser Coupling – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Glaser coupling describing oxidative homocoupling and heterocoupling of terminal alkynes to furnish conjugated diynes under copper-mediated aerobic conditions and related Hay, Eglinton, and palladium-assisted variants.
notes: Key concepts: copper acetylide formation, oxidative dimerization, macrocyclization of diynes, heterocoupling logic, and solvent/ligand effects that tune homocoupling versus cross-coupling outcomes.

entities.notes: Named oxidative alkyne-coupling family overview.
Copper(II)-acetate-mediated modification highlighted on the overview page.
CuCl/TMEDA/O2 variant for diyne formation shown on the overview page.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidative coupling of terminal alkynes to diynes
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidative alkyne-coupling family widely used for diyne synthesis, macrocyclization, and heterocoupling variants in complex-molecule synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: metal-mediated coupling of organohalides to carbon-carbon bonds
similar_existing.description_short: Classical sodium-mediated coupling of organohalides used for dimerization, cross-coupling, and intramolecular ring closure of suitable dihalides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd- or Ni-catalyzed cross-coupling of organozinc reagents with organic electrophiles
similar_existing.description_short: Organozinc-based cross-coupling method valued for high chemoselectivity and compatibility with sensitive functional groups.

similar_existing.transformation_type: low-valent titanium-mediated reductive coupling of carbonyl compounds to alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Reductive olefination method that couples carbonyl compounds under low-valent titanium conditions and is especially powerful for macrocyclization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed coupling of terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl halides
similar_existing.description_short: Cross-coupling reaction joining terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl electrophiles under palladium and copper catalysis.

title: Grignard Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Grignard reaction describing preparation and reactivity of organomagnesium reagents toward carbonyl compounds, nitriles, carbon dioxide, acid derivatives, and epoxides in aprotic ethereal solvents.
notes: Key concepts: organomagnesium-halide generation, carbon nucleophilicity, moisture sensitivity, chelation and Schlenk-type behavior, and concerted versus radical-stepwise models for addition to electrophiles.

entities.notes: Named organomagnesium addition family overview.
Reactive organometallic carbon nucleophile generated from an organohalide and magnesium.
Organomagnesium speciation concept explicitly mentioned in the overview discussion.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nucleophilic carbon-carbon bond formation with organomagnesium reagents
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational organometallic addition chemistry in which organomagnesium reagents act as strongly nucleophilic carbon equivalents toward diverse electrophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed coupling of alkenes with aldehydes to form oxocarbenium-derived oxygen heterocycles and alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Versatile acid-promoted alkene–aldehyde coupling that channels oxocarbenium chemistry into tetrahydropyrans, 1,3-dioxanes, allylic alcohols, and related oxygenated products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or azlactone precursors to α,β-unsaturated acids or azlactones
similar_existing.description_short: Classical olefination manifold that converts aromatic aldehydes into cinnamic-acid-type products or azlactone intermediates using anhydride-based carbon nucleophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-promoted conversion of nitriles and carbocations into amides
similar_existing.description_short: Classical carbocation-trapping reaction in which nitriles are converted into amides under strongly acidic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted conversion of glycals or enol ethers to unsaturated glycosides or cyclohexanones
similar_existing.description_short: Lewis-acid-promoted rearrangement chemistry of unsaturated carbohydrates that affords 2,3-unsaturated glycosides or highly substituted cyclohexanones.

title: Grob Fragmentation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Grob fragmentation describing stereoelectronically controlled heterolytic cleavage of suitably substituted frameworks to generate an electrofugal fragment, an unsaturated fragment, and a nucleofugal fragment in a concerted or stepwise fashion.
notes: Key concepts: alignment requirements for syn fragmentation, one-step versus stepwise ionic pathways, dependence on electrofuge and nucleofuge quality, and preference for rigid polycyclic substrates.

entities.notes: Named stereoelectronic fragmentation family overview.
Concerted Grob-fragmentation pathway shown on the mechanism page.
Stepwise cationic or anionic variants discussed alongside the concerted pathway.

similar_existing.transformation_type: stereoelectronically controlled fragmentation to unsaturated products
similar_existing.description_short: Fragmentation family that cleaves rigid, properly aligned precursors into unsaturated fragments under strong-base, alkoxide, or related triggering conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-induced fragmentation of 1,3-diol derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Base-induced stereospecific fragmentation of 1,3-diol monotosylates or related substrates to generate unsaturated fragments, often medium-ring alkenes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: fragmentation of epoxyketone hydrazones to alkynones / alkynals
similar_existing.description_short: Fragmentation of epoxy ketone hydrazones that furnishes alkynones or alkynals through a nitrogen-expelling Grob-type process.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Michael addition followed by intramolecular aldol condensation to cyclohexenones
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational annulation strategy that builds cyclohexenones by combining Michael addition and intramolecular aldol chemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: beta-keto ester / dicarbonyl cleavage
similar_existing.description_short: Mentioned in color-explanation front matter on page xiv.
